articles.title: Nasdaq seen rising but Dow Jones falling as Trump lands in China

articles.title: Trump, Xi to weigh tariff cuts on $30 billion of imports in managed trade push
articles.excerpt: The U.S. and China are expected to inch toward a managed trade mechanism for non-sensitive goods this week, with each side possibly identifying some $30 billion worth of goods on ​which they could reduce tariffs and sell to each other without crossing national security red lines.

articles.title: SMA Solar sales rise as Europe turns to rooftop panels
articles.excerpt: SMA Solar said on Thursday demand for residential and commercial solar equipment in Europe has risen since the start ​of the Iran war, lifting sales at its home and ‌business division.

articles.title: Shiller P/E Hits Dot-Com Bubble Levels: Warning Or Noise?
articles.excerpt: The S&P 500's Shiller P/E (CAPE) ratio is near dot-com era highs, signaling potential overvaluation. While CAPE's predictive power is debated, current forward P/E of 21x also suggests elevated growth expectations and valuation risk.

articles.title: Morning Call Sheet: Markets weigh inflation, AI and rate fears

articles.title: Sizzling semiconductor trade at risk of cooling - and stalling US stocks rally
articles.excerpt: A stunning run-up in shares of semiconductor companies has helped drive the U.S. stock rally, but the eye-popping gains are sparking concerns about an overheated market and prompting some investors to prepare ​for a pullback.

articles.title: Big investors are trading private credit gains for infrastructure bets. Here's where else they're putting money.
articles.excerpt: Private credit's share of institutional portfolios has fallen, according to a report. Canoe Intelligence found that only infrastructure had net inflows from institutions in Q4 2025.
